975	(`Adjunction.homEquiv_counit`) and Seam 3. -/
976	
977	-- The conjugate-unit calculus chains several `erw` defeq-unifications and a `simp` closure over the
978	-- `restrictScalars`/tilde–Γ round trips, which collectively exceed the default heartbeat budget.
979	set_option maxHeartbeats 4000000 in
980	/-- **Seam 1: the affine pullback–pushforward unit is the algebraic unit.** Let
981	`ι_A : A → A ⊗_R R'` be the canonical inclusion. The unit of the
982	`((Spec ι_A)^*, (Spec ι_A)_*)`-adjunction evaluated at `tilde M`, read on global sections over
983	`Spec A` through the two tilde dictionaries (`pullback_spec_tilde_iso`,
984	`pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`) and the tilde–Γ unit, equals the algebraic unit
985	`η_M : M → (A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M`, `m ↦ (1 ⊗ 1) ⊗ m` (Mathlib's `ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj`
986	unit). See blueprint `lem:base_change_mate_unit_value`. -/
987	theorem base_change_mate_unit_value {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}}
988	    (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
989	    letI : Algebra (R : Type u) (A : Type u) := φ.hom.toAlgebra
990	    letI : Algebra (R : Type u) (R' : Type u) := ψ.hom.toAlgebra
991	    let inclA := CommRingCat.ofHom (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom
992	      (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u)) (B := (R' : Type u)))
993	    (tilde.toTildeΓNatIso.app M).hom ≫
994	      (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := A)).map
995	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map inclA)).unit.app (tilde M)) ≫
996	        ((moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := A)).mapIso
997	            ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map inclA)).mapIso
998	                (pullback_spec_tilde_iso inclA M) ≪≫
999	              pushforward_spec_tilde_iso inclA ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M))
1000	          ≪≫ (tilde.toTildeΓNatIso.app
1001	                ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars inclA.hom).obj
1002	                  ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M))).symm).hom
1003	        = (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj inclA.hom).unit.app M := by
1004	  -- REMAINING (the affine, square-free heart): a conjugate-adjunction unit coherence. The
1005	  -- geometric adjunction `((Spec ι_A)^* ⊣ (Spec ι_A)_*)` transports, under the two tilde
1006	  -- dictionaries — `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` (identifying `(Spec ι_A)^*` of a tilde with
1007	  -- `extendScalars ι_A`) and `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` (identifying `(Spec ι_A)_*` of a tilde
1008	  -- with `restrictScalars ι_A`) — to the algebraic adjunction
1009	  -- `(extendScalars ι_A ⊣ restrictScalars ι_A) = ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj ι_A`. Since
1010	  -- `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` is by construction `((conjugateIsoEquiv adjL adjR).symm
1011	  -- (gammaPushforwardNatIso ι_A)).symm.app`, the LHS is the image of the geometric unit under the
1012	  -- comparison, and `Adjunction.conjugateEquiv` / `homEquiv` naturality identify it with the
1013	  -- algebraic unit evaluated at `M` (`m ↦ (1 ⊗ 1) ⊗ m`). The element-level actions of the two
1014	  -- dictionaries are opaque (built via `conjugateIsoEquiv`), so the closure needs the abstract
1015	  -- conjugate-unit coherence, not an `ext`-chase. Uses: `pullback_spec_tilde_iso`,
1016	  -- `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`, `Adjunction.conjugateEquiv`.
1017	  intro inclA
1018	  -- The two composed adjunctions, exactly as in `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` (with `φ := inclA`).
1019	  set adjL := (tilde.adjunction (R := A)).comp
1020	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map inclA)) with hadjL
1021	  set adjR := (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj inclA.hom).comp
1022	    (tilde.adjunction (R := _)) with hadjR
1023	  -- Move 1: the first two factors are the unit of `adjL`.
1024	  have hunitL : adjL.unit.app M
1025	      = (tilde.toTildeΓNatIso.app M).hom ≫
1026	          (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := A)).map
1027	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map inclA)).unit.app (tilde M)) := by
1028	    rw [hadjL, Adjunction.comp_unit_app]
1029	    rfl
1030	  -- Move 3: the unit of `adjR` splits off the algebraic unit `η_M`.
1031	  have hunitR : adjR.unit.app M
1032	      = (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj inclA.hom).unit.app M ≫
1033	          (ModuleCat.restrictScalars inclA.hom).map
1034	            ((tilde.toTildeΓNatIso.app ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M)).hom) := by
1035	    rw [hadjR, Adjunction.comp_unit_app]
1036	    rfl
1037	  -- The right-adjoint comparison nat-iso `β : R₁ ≅ R₂` fed to the conjugate calculus.
1038	  set β := gammaPushforwardNatIso inclA with hβ
1039	  -- Move 2: the conjugate-unit coherence. `((conjugateEquiv adjL adjR).symm β.hom).app M` is, by the
1040	  -- definition of `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` via `conjugateIsoEquiv`, exactly `pullback_spec_tilde_iso⁻¹`.
1041	  have hpullinv : ((conjugateEquiv adjL adjR).symm β.hom).app M
1042	      = (pullback_spec_tilde_iso inclA M).inv := by rw [hβ]; rfl
1043	  have huce := CategoryTheory.unit_conjugateEquiv_symm adjL adjR β.hom M
1044	  rw [hpullinv] at huce
1045	  -- Move 4, part 1 (Claim A): the pushforward dictionary `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`, read on Γ and
1046	  -- composed with the tilde–Γ unit, is the Γ-fragment comparison `gammaPushforwardTildeIso`. This is
1047	  -- the right-triangle identity of the tilde ⊣ Γ adjunction (`fromTildeΓ` is its counit).
1048	  have htri : (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := A)).map (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ
1049	        ((pushforward (Spec.map inclA)).obj (tilde ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M))))
1050	      = (tilde.toTildeΓNatIso.app ((moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := A)).obj
1051	          ((pushforward (Spec.map inclA)).obj
1052	            (tilde ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M))))).inv :=
1053	    (Iso.hom_comp_eq_id _).mp (tilde.adjunction.right_triangle_components _)
1054	  have hClaimA : (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := A)).map
1055	        (pushforward_spec_tilde_iso inclA ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M)).hom ≫
1056	        (tilde.toTildeΓNatIso.app ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars inclA.hom).obj
1057	          ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M))).inv
1058	      = (gammaPushforwardTildeIso inclA ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M)).hom := by
1059	    rw [Iso.comp_inv_eq, pushforward_spec_tilde_iso, pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_of_isLocalizedModule]
1060	    simp only [Iso.trans_hom, Iso.symm_hom, asIso_inv, Functor.mapIso_hom, Functor.map_comp,
1061	      Functor.map_inv, IsIso.inv_comp_eq]
1062	    rw [htri]
1063	    exact (NatIso.naturality_1 tilde.toTildeΓNatIso
1064	      (gammaPushforwardTildeIso inclA ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M)).hom).symm
1065	  -- Move 4, part 2: `β.hom` at a tilde object is the per-object `gammaPushforwardIso`, and
1066	  -- `gammaPushforwardTildeIso` factors through it and the tilde–Γ unit.
1067	  have hβapp : β.hom.app (tilde ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M))
1068	      = (gammaPushforwardIso inclA (tilde ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M))).hom := by
1069	    rw [hβ, gammaPushforwardNatIso]; simp
1070	  have hgPTI : (gammaPushforwardTildeIso inclA ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M)).hom
1071	      = β.hom.app (tilde ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M)) ≫
1072	        (ModuleCat.restrictScalars inclA.hom).map
1073	          (tilde.toTildeΓNatIso.app ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M)).inv := by
1074	    rw [hβapp, gammaPushforwardTildeIso]
1075	    simp [Iso.trans_hom]
1076	  -- Final assembly. Move 1 folds the first two factors into `adjL.unit`; unfolding the bracket and
1077	  -- applying Claim A + hgPTI rewrites it through `β`; β-naturality at `pullback_spec_tilde_iso.hom`
1078	  -- and the conjugate-unit identity `huce` then collapse everything to the algebraic unit `η_M`.
1079	  rw [← Category.assoc, ← hunitL]
1080	  simp only [Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, Iso.symm_hom, Functor.map_comp, Category.assoc]
1081	  rw [hClaimA]
1082	  -- Finish: hgPTI expands the Γ-fragment comparison; converting to composed-functor form
1083	  -- (`← Functor.comp_map`) lets the β-naturality square (`erw [β.hom.naturality_assoc]`) and the
1084	  -- conjugate-unit identity (`erw [reassoc_of% huce]`) push the geometric unit through `β`; the
1085	  -- tilde–Γ and pullback-iso round trips then cancel, leaving the algebraic unit `η_M` (hunitR).
1086	  rw [hgPTI]
1087	  simp only [← Functor.comp_map]
1088	  erw [β.hom.naturality_assoc]
1089	  erw [reassoc_of% huce]
1090	  rw [hunitR]
1091	  simp only [Functor.comp_map]
1092	  simp [← Functor.map_comp]
1093	  rw [← Iso.app_hom, ← Iso.app_inv, Iso.hom_inv_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, Category.comp_id]

1095	/-- **The `Spec R`-section reading `ρ` of the inner pushforward comparison.** The canonical
1096	`R`-linear map `M → (A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M`, `m ↦ (1 ⊗ 1) ⊗ m`, viewed as a morphism of `ModuleCat R`
1097	into the `restrictScalars ψ`/`restrictScalars inclR'` packaging of the codomain read. It is built
1098	as `restrictScalars φ` of the algebraic unit `η_M = extendRestrictScalarsAdj inclA` (Seam 1's
1099	value), transported across the ring equation `inclA ∘ φ = inclR' ∘ ψ` (both equal
1100	`algebraMap R (A ⊗_R R')`) by the change-of-rings tower isos. This is the `ρ` appearing on the RHS
1101	of Seam 2 (`base_change_mate_fstar_reindex`). -/
1102	noncomputable def base_change_mate_inner_value {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}}
1103	    (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
1104	    letI : Algebra (R : Type u) (A : Type u) := φ.hom.toAlgebra
1105	    letI : Algebra (R : Type u) (R' : Type u) := ψ.hom.toAlgebra
1106	    let inclA := CommRingCat.ofHom (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom
1107	      (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u)) (B := (R' : Type u)))
1108	    let inclR' := CommRingCat.ofHom (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight
1109	      (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u)) (B := (R' : Type u))).toRingHom
1110	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M ⟶
1111	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars ψ.hom).obj
1112	        ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars inclR'.hom).obj
1113	          ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M)) := by
1114	  letI : Algebra (R : Type u) (A : Type u) := φ.hom.toAlgebra
1115	  letI : Algebra (R : Type u) (R' : Type u) := ψ.hom.toAlgebra
1116	  intro inclA inclR'
1117	  have hring : inclA.hom.comp φ.hom = inclR'.hom.comp ψ.hom := by
1118	    ext r
1119	    show (CommRingCat.Hom.hom φ) r ⊗ₜ[(R : Type u)] (1 : (R' : Type u))
1120	        = (1 : (A : Type u)) ⊗ₜ[(R : Type u)] (CommRingCat.Hom.hom ψ) r
1121	    have ha : (CommRingCat.Hom.hom φ) r = r • (1 : (A : Type u)) :=
1122	      Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one r
1123	    have hb : (CommRingCat.Hom.hom ψ) r = r • (1 : (R' : Type u)) :=
1124	      Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one r
1125	    rw [ha, hb, TensorProduct.smul_tmul]
1126	  exact (ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).map
1127	        ((ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj inclA.hom).unit.app M) ≫
1128	      ((ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App φ.hom inclA.hom (inclA.hom.comp φ.hom) rfl
1129	            ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M)).symm ≪≫
1130	        (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsCongr hring).app
1131	            ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M) ≪≫
1132	        ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App ψ.hom inclR'.hom (inclR'.hom.comp ψ.hom) rfl
1133	            ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M)).hom

1135	/-- **Pseudofunctoriality of the pullback–pushforward unit.** For composable scheme morphisms
1136	`a : X₁ ⟶ X₂`, `b : X₂ ⟶ X₃` and a module `N` on `X₁`, the unit of the
1137	`(pullback (a ≫ b) ⊣ pushforward (a ≫ b))`-adjunction factors through the units of `a` and `b`
1138	together with the `pushforwardComp`/`pullbackComp` coherences. This is the abstract mate identity
1139	`unit_conjugateEquiv` specialised by `conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv`
1140	(`conjugateEquiv … (pullbackComp a b).inv = (pushforwardComp a b).hom`). It is the leg-reindex
1141	engine of Seam 2: with `a := e.hom` (an iso) and `b := Spec ι_A`, it turns the generic
1142	`(pullback.fst)`-unit into the affine `(Spec ι_A)`-unit modulo the transparent coherences.
1143	Project-local. -/
1144	theorem pullbackPushforward_unit_comp {X₁ X₂ X₃ : Scheme.{u}} (a : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (b : X₂ ⟶ X₃)
1145	    (N : X₃.Modules) :
1146	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app N ≫
1147	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map
1148	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app
1149	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj N)) ≫
1150	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp a b).hom.app _
1151	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (a ≫ b)).unit.app N ≫
1152	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (a ≫ b)).map
1153	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a b).inv.app N) := by
1154	  have h := CategoryTheory.unit_conjugateEquiv
1155	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).comp
1156	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a))
1157	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (a ≫ b))
1158	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a b).inv N
1159	  rw [Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv, Adjunction.comp_unit_app] at h
1160	  rw [← Category.assoc]
1161	  exact h

1163	/-! ### Seam 2, step (ii): Γ-collapse of the transparent pushforward coherences
1164	
1165	On global sections over `Spec R`, the two `pushforwardComp` composition coherences and the
1166	`pushforwardCongr` congruence coherence appearing in the inner composite `θ_in` are transparent:
1167	their section value at every open is the identity (`pushforwardComp_*_app_app = 𝟙`) or a presheaf
1168	transport (`pushforwardCongr_hom_app_app`). Hence under `moduleSpecΓFunctor` they collapse to the
1169	identity / an `eqToHom` repackaging. These are the blueprint step-(ii) atomic claims. -/
1170	
1171	/-- **(ii-a) Γ-collapse of `pushforwardComp` (hom factor).** The `pushforwardComp` hom-coherence
1172	has identity section value at every open, hence is the identity morphism of `(Spec R).Modules`, and
1173	its `moduleSpecΓFunctor` image is the identity. -/
1174	private lemma gammaMap_pushforwardComp_hom_eq_id {X₁ X₂ : Scheme.{u}} {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
1175	    (a : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (b : X₂ ⟶ Spec R) (M : X₁.Modules) :
1176	    (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R)).map
1177	      ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp a b).hom.app M) = 𝟙 _ := by
1178	  have h : (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp a b).hom.app M = 𝟙 _ := rfl
1179	  rw [h]; exact (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R)).map_id _
1180	
1181	/-- **(ii-b) Γ-collapse of `pushforwardComp` (inv factor).** Same as (ii-a) for the inverse. -/
1182	private lemma gammaMap_pushforwardComp_inv_eq_id {X₁ X₂ : Scheme.{u}} {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
1183	    (a : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (b : X₂ ⟶ Spec R) (M : X₁.Modules) :
1184	    (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R)).map
1185	      ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp a b).inv.app M) = 𝟙 _ := by
1186	  have h : (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp a b).inv.app M = 𝟙 _ := rfl

1261	
1262	The five lemmas `base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_{unitExpand, gammaDistribute, eCancel,
1263	affineUnit, innerMatch}` decompose the step-(iii) obligation of

1267	/-- **(iii-1) Unit expansion: inverting the comp-coherence.** For composable `a : X₁ ⟶ X₂`,
1268	`b : X₂ ⟶ X₃`, a module `N` on `X₃`, and `g' = a ≫ b`, the bare `(g')`-unit expands as the
1269	four-factor composite `η^b ≫ b_*(η^a) ≫ pushforwardComp(a,b).hom ≫ g'_*(pullbackComp(a,b).hom)`.
1270	This is the inversion of the leg-reindex engine `pullbackPushforward_unit_comp`: post-composing its
1271	identity with `g'_*(pullbackComp.hom)` collapses the trailing `pullbackComp.inv` (functoriality of
1272	`g'_*` on `inv ≫ hom = id`). See blueprint `lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand`. -/
1273	theorem base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand {X₁ X₂ X₃ : Scheme.{u}}
1274	    (a : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (b : X₂ ⟶ X₃) (N : X₃.Modules) :
1275	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (a ≫ b)).unit.app N
1276	      = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app N ≫
1277	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map
1278	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app
1279	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj N)) ≫
1280	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp a b).hom.app
1281	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj N))) ≫
1282	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (a ≫ b)).map
1283	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a b).hom.app N) := by
1284	  rw [pullbackPushforward_unit_comp a b N]
1285	  -- The two trailing `(a ≫ b)_*`-images cancel (`pullbackComp.inv ≫ pullbackComp.hom = 𝟙`), but a
1286	  -- spurious `X.Modules` instance diamond under single-file elaboration makes `rw [Category.assoc]`
1287	  -- / `rw [hI]` / `simp only [Iso.inv_hom_id_app]` misfire on the goal's locked composition. We
1288	  -- prove the cancellation on FRESH terms (where the rewrites match) and discharge the goal in
1289	  -- term mode (`exact`), which checks defeq and tolerates the diamond.
1290	  have hI : (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (a ≫ b)).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a b).inv.app N) ≫
1291	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (a ≫ b)).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a b).hom.app N)
1292	      = 𝟙 _ := by
1293	    rw [← Functor.map_comp, Iso.inv_hom_id_app, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
1294	  have hfin := (congrArg (fun z =>
1295	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (a ≫ b)).unit.app N ≫ z) hI).trans
1296	    (Category.comp_id _)
1297	  exact ((Category.assoc _ _ _).trans hfin).symm

1300	functor `F` out of `X₃.Modules` to the `unitExpand` four-factor expansion of the `(a ≫ b)`-unit
1301	distributes over the four factors, by functoriality (`F (u ≫ v) = F u ≫ F v`). In the assembly `F`
1302	is `(Spec φ)_* ⋙ Γ_R`, giving the four `Γ`-image factors of the blueprint. See blueprint
1303	`lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_gammaDistribute`. -/
1304	theorem base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_gammaDistribute {X₁ X₂ X₃ : Scheme.{u}}
1305	    {D : Type*} [Category D] (a : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (b : X₂ ⟶ X₃) (N : X₃.Modules)
1306	    (F : X₃.Modules ⥤ D) :
1307	    F.map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (a ≫ b)).unit.app N)
1308	      = (F.map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app N) ≫
1309	          F.map ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map
1310	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app
1311	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj N))) ≫
1312	          F.map ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp a b).hom.app
1313	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj N)))) ≫
1314	        F.map ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (a ≫ b)).map
1315	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a b).hom.app N)) := by
1316	  -- After `unitExpand` the goal is pure functoriality (`F (u ≫ v) = F u ≫ F v`), but the
1317	  -- `X.Modules` instance diamond blocks `rw/simp [Functor.map_comp]`; we apply `F.map_comp` in term
1318	  -- mode (elaboration unifies the instances up to defeq).
1319	  rw [base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand a b N]
1320	  exact (F.map_comp _ _).trans (congrArg (· ≫ F.map _)
1321	    ((F.map_comp _ _).trans (congrArg (F.map _ ≫ ·) (F.map_comp _ _))))

1868	  \emph{Step (ii): \(\Gamma\)-collapse of the transparent coherences.} On global sections the
1869	  inverse pushforward-composition factor collapses to the identity

1875	  \emph{Step (iii): the mate-unwinding crux, as a five-link chain.}
1876	  \begin{enumerate}
1877	    \item Expand the bare \((g')\)-unit by inverting the comp-coherence

1879	    \item Distribute the expansion through \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\) and \(\Gamma\)
1880	      (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_gammaDistribute}), exposing four
1881	      \(\Gamma\)-image factors.
1882	    \item Cancel the two \(e\)-factors and the surviving
1883	      \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g',\operatorname{Spec}\varphi})^{\mathrm{hom}}\) against the matching
1884	      \(e\)-pieces inside the codomain read
1885	      (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_eCancel}), leaving only the affine
1886	      \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)\)-unit over the affine codomain remnant.
1887	    \item Evaluate that affine unit through the tilde/\(\Gamma\) dictionaries as the restriction of
1888	      scalars along \(\varphi\) of the algebraic unit \(\eta_M\)
1889	      (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_affineUnit}, Seam~1).
1890	    \item Read it over \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) by restriction of scalars along \(\psi\) and the ring
1891	      equation \(\iota_A \circ \varphi = \iota_{R'} \circ \psi\) to land on \(\rho\)
1892	      (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_innerMatch},
1893	      Definition~\ref{def:base_change_mate_inner_value}).
1894	  \end{enumerate}
1895	  Composing the five links proves the identity; link~(3) is the load-bearing telescoping.
1896	\end{proof}

1991	  \(g' = \operatorname{pr}_1\), \(f' = \operatorname{pr}_2\): with the codomain read
1992	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read} being definitionally the variable-legs read at
1993	  those legs, the concrete statement follows directly. The remainder of this proof records the
1994	  mathematical content of that abstract identity.
1995	
1996	  Throughout, let \(e : X' \xrightarrow{\sim} \operatorname{Spec}(A \otimes_R R')\) be the
1997	  canonical isomorphism of Lemma~\ref{lem:pullbackSpecIso_mathlib}, and
1998	  \(\iota_A : A \to A \otimes_R R'\), \(\iota_{R'} : R' \to A \otimes_R R'\) the tensor
1999	  inclusions, so that by Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor} the two projection legs
2000	  factor as
2001	  \[
2002	    g' \;=\; e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A,
2003	    \qquad
2004	    f' \;=\; e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_{R'} .
2005	  \]
2006	  The inner composite \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\) is the four-factor map
2007	  \[
2008	    f_{*}\bigl(\eta^{g'}_{\widetilde M}\bigr) \;\circ\;
2009	    (\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g', f})^{\mathrm{hom}} \;\circ\;
2010	    (\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\,w)^{\mathrm{hom}} \;\circ\;
2011	    (\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{f', g})^{\mathrm{inv}},
2012	  \]
2013	  with \(f = \operatorname{Spec}\varphi\), \(g = \operatorname{Spec}\psi\),
2014	  \(\eta^{g'}\) the unit of the \((g')\)-adjunction, and
2015	  \(w : g' \circ f = f' \circ g\) the commutativity of the square.
2016	
2017	  \emph{Why a direct substitution fails.} The identity cannot be obtained by substituting the
2018	  leg factorizations directly, because the legs \(g'\) and \(f'\) appear in multiple positions
2019	  simultaneously: as the index of the adjunction \(\eta^{g'}\), inside the equality proof
2020	  \(w\) parametrizing \(\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\,w\), inside the argument
2021	  \(f'_{*}\bigl((g')^{*}\widetilde M\bigr)\) of the codomain comparison
2022	  \(\gamma_{\psi}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}), and inside the opaque codomain
2023	  identification \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}} = \) Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}.
2024	  The reindex is therefore restructured into three sub-steps so that the legs become explicit
2025	  free variables; it is \emph{not} a single rewrite.
2026	
2027	  \emph{(i) Abstract variable-legs restatement.} Restate the codomain read — and, with it, the
2028	  whole four-factor chain — for \emph{generic} morphisms \(g', f'\) carrying the cone-leg
2029	  equalities \(g' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\) and
2030	  \(f' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_{R'}\) as explicit hypotheses, in place of the literal
2031	  projections \(\operatorname{pr}_1, \operatorname{pr}_2\). In this abstract form the legs are
2032	  free variables, not terms pinned by the pullback's universal property, so each can be
2033	  eliminated by substituting its defining equation: the equality proof \(w\), the adjunction
2034	  index, and the \(\gamma_{\psi}\)/\(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\) arguments are all expressed in the
2035	  free leg variables and transform uniformly under the substitution. This restatement converts
2036	  the blocked rewrite into a legitimate one; it is the structural heart of the step.
2037	
2038	  \emph{(ii) \(\Gamma\)-collapse of the transparent coherences.} On global sections, read through
2039	  the \(\Gamma\)-pushforward dictionaries (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso},
2040	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}), the two pushforward-composition factors and the
2041	  congruence factor are transparent. The composition coherences collapse to the identity —
2042	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g',f})^{\mathrm{hom}}\) and
2043	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{f',g})^{\mathrm{inv}}\) evaluate, on the top open, to the identity
2044	  map on restriction of scalars (their section-level values are the identity) — and they
2045	  serve only to rebracket the iterated pushforward along the two factorizations
2046	  \((g' \circ f)_* = (f' \circ g)_*\) of the square's composite. (The
2047	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g',f})^{\mathrm{hom}}\) collapse is a transparent re-association
2048	  that is most naturally accounted for together with the leg-reindex of step~(iii) rather than at
2049	  this point; the \(\Gamma\)-collapse identity itself is the same.) The congruence factor collapses
2050	  to a canonical transport along the equality of opens: its section-level value
2051	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\,w)^{\mathrm{hom}}\) is the
2052	  presheaf-restriction along the canonical equality of opens, i.e.\ a repackaging along
2053	  that equality carrying no substantive content. Thus after the \(\Gamma\)-collapse the only
2054	  surviving non-trivial factor is \(f_{*}\bigl(\eta^{g'}_{\widetilde M}\bigr)\) read on global
2055	  sections.
2056	
2057	  \emph{(iii) Reduction to Seam~1 via the leg-reindex engine.} With the legs substituted
2058	  (step~(i)) to \(g' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\), the leg-reindex engine
2059	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pullbackPushforward_unit_comp}, instantiated at \(a = e\),
2060	  \(b = \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\), \(N = \widetilde M\), rewrites the unit factor
2061	  \(\eta^{g'} = \eta^{e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A}\), together with the
2062	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{e,\,\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A})\) coherence, into the affine
2063	  \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)\)-unit followed by \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)_{*}\) of the
2064	  \(e\)-unit. Since \(e\) is an isomorphism of schemes, its pullback functor is an equivalence
2065	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso}) and the \(e\)-unit is invertible, hence an
2066	  isomorphism absorbed into the codomain identification \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\). The surviving
2067	  \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)\)-unit has section value over \(\operatorname{Spec} A\) equal to
2068	  the algebraic unit \(\eta_M : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\) of Seam~1
2069	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}). Composing the surviving factor with the
2070	  codomain identification \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\)
2071	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}) and reading over \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) by
2072	  restriction of scalars along \(\psi\) identifies all four factors with the restriction of
2073	  scalars along \(\psi\) of the codomain-read transport of the unit value, namely
2074	  \(\rho : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\)
2075	  (Definition~\ref{def:base_change_mate_inner_value}) — the
2076	  ``\(X' = \operatorname{Spec}(R' \otimes_R A)\), \(\mathcal F'\) is
2077	  \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\)'' bookkeeping of the source. Each of (i)--(iii) extracts as a
2078	  named lemma; together they close the reindex without ever rewriting the bare legs.
2079	\end{proof}

2146	  \emph{Counit factorization.} The base-change map
2147	  (Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map}) is the adjunction transpose of
2148	  \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\) under \((g^* \dashv g_*)\) with \(g = \operatorname{Spec}\psi\). By the
2149	  counit form of the hom-set adjunction (\(\operatorname{homEquiv}\)-counit identity for the
2150	  transpose), it factors as \(g^*(\theta_{\mathrm{in}})\) followed by the counit
2151	  \(\varepsilon_g\), with no opaque transpose remaining:
2152	  \(\Gamma(\theta) = \Gamma\bigl(g^*(\theta_{\mathrm{in}})\bigr) \circ \Gamma(\varepsilon_g)\).
2153	
2154	  \emph{The concrete counit coherence — why it is not a bare computation.} Conjugating
2155	  \(g^*(\theta_{\mathrm{in}})\) followed by \(\varepsilon_g\) by the domain identification
2156	  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read}) and the codomain
2157	  identification \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read})
2158	  requires the pullback dictionary \(\Pi_{\psi}\) of
2159	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso} — which reads \(g^* = (\operatorname{Spec}\psi)^*\) of a
2160	  tilde as extension of scalars along \(\psi\) — to interact with the geometric counit
2161	  \(\varepsilon_g\). This interaction is \emph{not} a definitional reduction: \(\Pi_{\psi}\) is
2162	  built abstractly as the uniqueness-of-left-adjoints comparison, evaluated from the
2163	  natural-isomorphism of right adjoints \(\gamma_{\psi}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardNatIso})
2164	  via the conjugate-of-isomorphisms equivalence; concretely \(\Pi_{\psi}\) is the component at the
2165	  module of
2166	  \(\bigl(\operatorname{conjIso}(\mathrm{adj}_L, \mathrm{adj}_R)^{-1}(\gamma_{\psi})\bigr)^{-1}\),
2167	  where \(\mathrm{adj}_L = (\widetilde{(-)}_R \dashv \Gamma_R) . (g^* \dashv g_*)\) is the
2168	  composite ``tilde then pullback'' adjunction and
2169	  \(\mathrm{adj}_R = (\,{}^{\psi}\!(-) \dashv {}_{\psi}(-)) . (\widetilde{(-)}_{R'} \dashv
2170	  \Gamma_{R'})\) is the composite ``extension of scalars then tilde'' adjunction. Because the
2171	  dictionary is opaque in this way, the counit cannot be simplified against it by reflexivity
2172	  or by definitional unfolding; the identification must be made through the abstract conjugate calculus.
2173	
2174	  \emph{Conjugation via the composed-adjunction counit-triangle identity.} The required identity
2175	  is the counit-triangle (zig-zag) coherence for the \emph{composed} adjunction
2176	  \((\widetilde{(-)}_{R'} \dashv \Gamma_{R'}) . (g^* \dashv g_*)\), the same composite adjunction
2177	  whose left adjoint \(\Pi_{\psi}\) compares. Read concretely, this coherence states that the
2178	  dictionary \(\Pi_{\psi}\), composed with the geometric counit \(\varepsilon_g\) and the
2179	  tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit, equals the algebraic counit of the
2180	  extension/restriction-of-scalars adjunction \(({}^{\psi}\!(-) \dashv {}_{\psi}(-))\) along
2181	  \(\psi\); equivalently, conjugating \(\Gamma(\varepsilon_g)\) by the tilde dictionaries
2182	  identifies it with the module-level adjunction counit of
2183	  the extension/restriction-of-scalars adjunction along \(\psi\). This is the dual companion of the
2184	  unit-across-conjugate coherence (Lemma~\ref{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_mathlib}) used in Seam~1 and
2185	  Seam~2. Applying it, conjugation replaces the transpose by extension of scalars along \(\psi\)
2186	  applied to the \(\operatorname{Spec} R\)-section reading \(\rho\) of \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\)
2187	  from Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex} (Seam~2): the geometric counit collapses to
2188	  the module-level counit, and the residual \(g^*\)-leg becomes \({}^{\psi}\!(-) = \) extension of
2189	  scalars along \(\psi\) applied to \(\rho\).
2190	
2191	  \emph{Identification on generators.} Since \(\rho : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\), its
2192	  extension of scalars along \(\psi\) sends
2193	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto r' \cdot ((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m) = (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\). This
2194	  is precisely the inverse of the regrouping isomorphism
2195	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}) on generators, and both maps are
2196	  \(R'\)-linear, so they coincide.
2197	\end{proof}

2593	\begin{lemma}[Flat base change preserves finite equalizers]
2594	  \label{lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}
2595	  \lean{LinearMap.tensorEqLocusEquiv}
2596	  \mathlibok
2597	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
2598	  Let \(A \to B\) be a flat ring homomorphism (more generally, let \(B\) be a flat
2599	  \(A\)-module that is also an \(A\)-algebra, with the scalar-tower compatibility),
2600	  and let \(f, g : N \rightrightarrows P\) be a pair of \(A\)-linear maps. Write
2601	  \(\operatorname{eq}(f, g) = \{x \in N : f(x) = g(x)\}\) for their equalizer. Then the
2602	  canonical \(B\)-linear comparison map
2603	  \[
2604	    B \otimes_A \operatorname{eq}(f, g)
2605	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
2606	    \operatorname{eq}\bigl(\mathbf{1}_B \otimes f,\ \mathbf{1}_B \otimes g\bigr)
2607	  \]
2608	  is an isomorphism; that is, base change along a flat algebra commutes with the
2609	  formation of the finite equalizer (equivalently, of the kernel: the case
2610	  \(g = 0\) is \texttt{LinearMap.tensorKerEquiv}, underlain by
2611	  \texttt{Module.Flat.ker\_lTensor\_eq} and \texttt{Module.Flat.eqLocus\_lTensor\_eq}).

2613	  \(- \otimes_A B\) carries the degree-\(0\)/\(1\) equalizer
2614	  \(\prod_i \Gamma(U_i, \mathcal{F}) \rightrightarrows
2615	    \prod_{i,j} \Gamma(U_{ij}, \mathcal{F})\) of a finite cover to the corresponding
2616	  equalizer of the base-changed cover.
